revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''臼塚古墳'''（うすづかこふん）は、[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]大字稲田にある[[古墳]]。1948年9月10日に大分県の[[史跡]]に指定...'
revision.text:
'''臼塚古墳'''（うすづかこふん）は、[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]大字稲田にある[[古墳]]。1948年9月10日に大分県の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == 臼杵市北部を流れる熊崎川流域の台地上にある、全長約67m、後円部径約45m、高さ約6m、前方部幅約45mの臼杵地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[5世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
墳丘上には[[明治時代]]に臼杵神社の社殿が建立されており、このために墳丘の一部が切削されているが、全体としては原形をよくとどめている。前方部と後円部の境には、元来2基の石甲が立てられていた。これらの石甲は1976年6月5日に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、現在は臼杵神社境内の参道脇に覆屋を設けて移設されている。
臼杵神社建立時に、後円部から2基の舟形石棺が発見されており、各石棺からはそれぞれ2体及び1体の人骨が発見されている。これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡及び変形獣帯鏡の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、貝輪、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。
== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、石人、石馬のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.e-obs.com/heo/heodata/n65.htm 臼塚古墳（うすづかこふん）] - 大分歴史事典 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/usuzukasekijin.htm ふるさと再発見 臼塚の石人] - 臼杵市 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/kojinkotu.htm ふるさと再発見 古人骨は履歴書] - 臼杵市
{{DEFAULTSORT:うすつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:臼杵市]]

'''臼塚古墳'''（うすづかこふん）は、[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]大字稲田にある[[古墳]]。1948年9月10日に大分県の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == 臼杵市北部を流れる熊崎川流域の[[洪積台地]]（通称、三重野台地）の標高約20mの台地南端にある、全長約87m、後円部径約45m、高さ約6m、前方部幅約45mの臼杵地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[5世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
墳丘上には[[明治時代]]に臼杵神社の現在の社殿が建築されており、このために墳丘の一部が切削されているが、全体としては原形をよくとどめている。前方部と後円部の境には、元来2基の石甲（いずれも135cm）が立てられていた。これらの石甲は1976年6月5日に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、現在は臼杵神社境内の参道脇に覆屋を設けて移設されている。
臼杵神社の現在の社殿建築時に、後円部から大小2基の舟形石棺（全長285cm及び225cm）が発見されており、大分県史蹟名勝天然記念物調査会「史蹟名勝天然記念物調査報告第６号」（昭和３年）の記録では、各石棺は、一枚岩をくりぬいて造られ、内面全体に朱が施されており、それぞれ2体の人骨が埋葬されていたとしている。
これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。 また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡（直径9.9cm）及び変形獣帯鏡（直径9.7cm）の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、貝輪、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。なお、鏡はいずれも伯載鏡である。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。
== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、石人、石馬のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.e-obs.com/heo/heodata/n65.htm 臼塚古墳（うすづかこふん）] - 大分歴史事典 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/usuzukasekijin.htm ふるさと再発見 臼塚の石人] - 臼杵市 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/kojinkotu.htm ふるさと再発見 古人骨は履歴書] - 臼杵市
{{DEFAULTSORT:うすつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:臼杵市]]

これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。 また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡（直径9.9cm）及び変形獣帯鏡（直径9.7cm）の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、[[貝輪]]、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。なお、鏡はいずれも伯載鏡である。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。
== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、石人、石馬のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.e-obs.com/heo/heodata/n65.htm 臼塚古墳（うすづかこふん）] - 大分歴史事典 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/usuzukasekijin.htm ふるさと再発見 臼塚の石人] - 臼杵市 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/kojinkotu.htm ふるさと再発見 古人骨は履歴書] - 臼杵市

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ [[臼杵神社]]へのリンク
revision.text:
'''臼塚古墳'''（うすづかこふん）は、[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]大字稲田にある[[古墳]]。1948年9月10日に大分県の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == 臼杵市北部を流れる熊崎川流域の[[洪積台地]]（通称、三重野台地）の標高約20mの台地南端にある、全長約87m、後円部径約45m、高さ約6m、前方部幅約45mの臼杵地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[5世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
墳丘上には[[明治時代]]に[[臼杵神社]]の現在の社殿が建築されており、このために墳丘の一部が切削されているが、全体としては原形をよくとどめている。前方部と後円部の境には、元来2基の石甲（いずれも高さ135cm）が立てられていた。これらの石甲は1976年6月5日に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、現在は臼杵神社境内の参道脇に覆屋を設けて移設されている。
臼杵神社の現在の社殿建築時に、後円部から大小2基の舟形石棺（全長285cm及び225cm）が発見されており、各石棺は、一枚岩をくりぬいて造られ、内面全体に朱が施されており、それぞれ2体の人骨が埋葬されていたとしている。（大分県史蹟名勝天然記念物調査会『史蹟名勝天然記念物調査報告第6号』参照）
これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。 また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡（直径9.9cm）及び変形獣帯鏡（直径9.7cm）の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、[[貝輪]]、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。なお、鏡はいずれも伯載鏡である。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。
== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、石人、石馬のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.e-obs.com/heo/heodata/n65.htm 臼塚古墳（うすづかこふん）] - 大分歴史事典 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/usuzukasekijin.htm ふるさと再発見 臼塚の石人] - 臼杵市 * [http://www2.city.usuki.oita.jp/kyouiku/mamoru/saihakken/simokita/kojinkotu.htm ふるさと再発見 古人骨は履歴書] - 臼杵市

== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、[[石人石馬]]のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (大分県)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。

'''臼塚古墳'''（うすづかこふん）は、[[大分県]][[臼杵市]]大字稲田にある[[古墳]]。1948年9月10日に大分県の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == 臼杵市北部を流れる熊崎川流域の[[洪積台地]]（通称、三重野台地）の標高約20mの台地南端にある、全長約87m、後円部径約45m、高さ約6m、前方部幅約45mの臼杵地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[5世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
墳丘上には[[明治時代]]に[[臼杵神社]]の現在の社殿が建築されており、このために墳丘の一部が切削されているが、全体としては原形をよくとどめている。前方部と後円部の境には、元来2基の[[石人石馬|石甲]]（いずれも高さ135cm）が立てられていた。これらの石甲は1976年6月5日に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、現在は臼杵神社境内の参道脇に覆屋を設けて移設されている。
臼杵神社の現在の社殿建築時に、後円部から大小2基の舟形石棺（全長285cm及び225cm）が発見されており、各石棺は、一枚岩をくりぬいて造られ、内面全体に朱が施されており、それぞれ2体の人骨が埋葬されていたとしている。（大分県史蹟名勝天然記念物調査会『史蹟名勝天然記念物調査報告第6号』参照）
これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。 また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡（直径9.9cm）及び変形獣帯鏡（直径9.7cm）の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、[[貝輪]]、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。なお、鏡はいずれも伯載鏡である。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。

全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。

== 概要 == 臼杵市北部を流れる熊崎川流域の[[洪積台地]]（通称、三重野台地）の標高約20mの台地南端にある、全長約87m、後円部径約45m、高さ約6m、前方部幅約45mの臼杵地方最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[5世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
墳丘上には[[明治時代]]に[[臼杵神社]]の現在の社殿が建築されており、このために墳丘の一部が切削されているが、全体としては原形をよくとどめている。前方部と後円部の境には、元来2基の[[石人石馬|石甲]]（いずれも高さ135cm）が立てられていた。これらの石甲は1976年6月5日に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、現在は臼杵神社境内の参道脇に覆屋を設けて移設されている。
臼杵神社の現在の社殿建築時に、後円部から大小2基の舟形石棺（全長285cm及び225cm）が発見されており、各石棺は、一枚岩をくりぬいて造られ、内面全体に朱が施されており、それぞれ2体の人骨が埋葬されていたとしている<ref>大分県史蹟名勝天然記念物調査会『史蹟名勝天然記念物調査報告第6号』</ref>。
これらの人骨の[[外耳]]には潜水によって生じることが多い外耳道骨腫が認められることから、被葬者は[[海人]]であったと推定されている。また、副葬品として、位至三公双龍鏡（直径9.9cm）及び変形獣帯鏡（直径9.7cm）の2面の鏡、鉄剣、鉄刀、鉄鉾、短甲等の武具や、[[貝輪]]、[[勾玉]]等の装身具が出土している。なお、鏡はいずれも伯載鏡である。
被葬者は、古墳の規模、副葬品、外耳の特徴から、この地域の海人の支配者層であると考えられている。
== 石甲 == 臼塚古墳に見られる石甲は、[[石人石馬]]のような石像の一種で、細い円柱状の台部に、2つの円錐を頂部でつなげたような形状の胴部が載る石製品であり、甲冑を着た人を象った石像であるとされる。ただし、頭部は作られていない。その形が臼と杵のようにも見えることから、臼杵の地名はこの石甲から起こったものであるとする説もある<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/eki008a1.pdf 各駅停車・大分県歴史散歩 ふるさとの駅 (8) 臼杵・津久見]}} 梅木秀徳、大分合同新聞社、2007年4月20日</ref>。
全国的に見ると、石人、石馬は、北部九州を中心に分布し、そのうち特に[[筑紫君磐井]]の墓に比定される福岡県の[[岩戸山古墳]]では、80基を超える石人、石馬が発見されている。大分県内では他に、同じく臼杵市の熊崎川流域にある[[下山古墳 (臼杵市)|下山古墳]]のみで見られるもので、北部九州の分布域から離れた[[豊後水道]]沿岸の臼杵にのみ分布していることが注目されている。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500356/ 臼塚古墳] - 臼杵市HP
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧#大分県]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient tomb | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{Location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=Location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader category of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]

{{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader category of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kk]][[category:most recent 500]][[ja:臼塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient tomb | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{Location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=Location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kk]][[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient tomb | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{Location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=Location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader category of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]]

Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]]

revision.comment: redirecting the alterrnative names all to this one name
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader category of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]]

Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:臼塚古墳]]

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[ja:臼塚古墳]]

Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] -
