(Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saddle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saqqle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}

{{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saqqle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Tumulus) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Tumulus == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Kofun == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saqqle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}

Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Kofun == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saqqle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}

significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Kofun == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a saqqle. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alias = Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | group = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | latitude = 35|27|7.05|N | longitude = 133|26|33.60|E | ISO = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | size = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, [[Sue ware]], [[Haniwa]] | satellites = | construction_period = Mid [[6th century]] | occupant = | tomb = | designation = National [[Historic Site]] (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_cultural_property = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of [[stone human and horse figures]] on Honshu | map2= {{location map|Japan Tottori|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]], and a stone horse excavated from the site is a designated National [[Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where [[stone human and horse figures]] (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in [[1989]] (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other artifacts include a stone horse and [[Sue ware]], with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-[[6th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was designated as part of a National [[Historic Site]] in [[1999]] (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]] in [[1959]] (Showa 34). == Chronology == * [[1902]] (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * [[1959]] (Showa 34): The stone horse was designated a National [[Important Cultural Property]]. * [[1989]] (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in [[1990]]. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): The site was designated as part of the National [[Historic Site]] "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Kofun == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == Artifacts == [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun Stone Horse (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a sa11le. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}

The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader Category:kk]][[Category: of "[[stone human and horse figures]]" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende [[andesite]] from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Informational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a sa11le. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwatoyama Kofun]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the [[Edo period]] and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, formerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == {{references}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Cultural Properties Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:ishiumadani kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yonago City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Usuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:臼塚古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Usuzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Usuzuka Kofun}}
